ranges.notes: Cold, often sub-zero temperatures and short daylight hours; very low tourist crowds. New Year's celebrations attract some visitors, but most attractions are quiet.
Very cold and often snow-covered; many outdoor attractions less accessible. Low activity in cultural events; fewer tourists but limited daylight and cold weather discourage visits.
Cold weather continues with occasional snow; low tourist numbers. Some indoor cultural events begin to pick up but weather remains a deterrent for most travelers.
Still cold but increasing daylight and romantic atmosphere around Valentine's Day improve appeal for couples. Some cultural events and cozy indoor venues attract visitors.
Transition to spring with milder temperatures; some rainy days. Tourist numbers start to rise but many outdoor attractions still quiet. Good for budget travelers seeking fewer crowds.
Warmer weather and Easter holidays bring moderate crowds. Traditional markets and cultural events create an engaging atmosphere; ideal for families and cultural tourists.
Spring in full bloom, temperatures rise to comfortable levels; light rainfall possible. Increasing outdoor activities and moderate tourist crowds make it a good month.

ranges.event: Gdańsk Easter Markets and cultural events
ranges.notes: Popular time for cultural festivals and markets; pleasant weather and growing tourist activity. Good balance of activities and manageable crowds.
Warm, pleasant weather with many outdoor festivities. National holidays bring vibrant local events, lively crowds, and great opportunities for sightseeing and cultural immersion.
Consistently warm and sunny; ideal for outdoor exploration and beach visits. Tourist season ramps up but the city still feels spacious and welcoming.
Start of summer with long daylight hours and warm but comfortable temperatures. Excellent for beach activities, sightseeing, and outdoor festivals; crowds increase but manageable.
One of the city's liveliest festivals with bonfires, music, and traditional celebrations. Warm weather and festive atmosphere attract many visitors; expect higher crowds.
Peak summer season with warmest temperatures, ideal for seaside and water activities. High tourist influx, lively nightlife, and numerous cultural events. Best for active travelers who enjoy crowds.
Warm weather continues with many open-air cultural events including the renowned Shakespeare Festival. Peak tourist season with lively atmosphere and full services.
Warm but starting to cool slightly late in the month. Tourist crowds begin to taper off but still considerable. Good time for those avoiding peak July congestion.
Early autumn with mild temperatures and fewer tourists. Ideal for cultural sightseeing and comfortable outdoor activities. Crowds reduce significantly compared to summer.

ranges.event: All Saints' Day preparations and local harvest festivals
ranges.notes: Cooling weather and some rainy days; local festivals provide cultural interest but fewer tourists. Good for travelers seeking authentic local experiences.
Autumn colors and cooler temperatures; increasing rainfall. Tourist numbers low but many attractions remain open. Good for budget travelers and those seeking a quieter visit.
Chillier weather and shorter days; some attractions begin to close early for winter. Low tourist activity and less outdoor appeal.
Cold, damp weather with very low sunlight; local religious observances create a solemn atmosphere. Tourist traffic low and many outdoor sites less accessible.
Cold and often grey with high chance of rain or early snow; very low tourism. Most outdoor activities not recommended.
Cold weather but festive Christmas markets and decorations enliven the city. Moderate tourist numbers with great opportunities for cultural experiences and shopping.
Festive atmosphere with special events and fireworks; cold but lively. Higher crowds around holidays, suitable for travelers who enjoy winter festivities.
